generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance RealTimeControlAutomation : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Implements autonomous decision-making via state machines (SysML v1.7, MontiArc Automaton) or code-based logic (C++, Java) to adjust physical system parameters or trigger corrective actions, closing the loop between digital and physical domains."
]

instance SimulationDrivenOptimization : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Applies algorithmic or model-based optimization using simulation-driven feedback loops (MathWorks Simulink, domain-specific simulation tools) to improve efficiency, reduce costs, or enhance reliability of the physical system."
]

instance MultiPerspectiveDashboardRenderer : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides interactive visualization of SysML diagrams, ADL visualizations, and aggregated system states for stakeholders (engineers, operators, managers) supporting diagnostics, planning, or maintenance scheduling."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components (implied by Digital Shadow integration)
instance PhysicalSystemContextualDataCollector : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects contextual data traces from the physical system including sensor readings, operational states, environmental parameters, or process metrics via unspecified communication protocols (event-triggered or time-based)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PhysicalSystemContextualDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance PhysicalSystemContextualDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PhysicalSystemContextualDataCollector
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance RequirementsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "System requirements layer modeled using MBSE methodologies (RFLP, OOSEM, SPES) capturing functional, non-functional, and stakeholder requirements."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingToolchain, VersionControlSystem
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Functional architecture layer decomposed using component-connector perspectives (SysML IBDs, AADL, MontiArc) representing system functions, interfaces, and interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingToolchain, MontiArcToolchain
]

instance LogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Logical architecture layer defining component implementations, software/hardware mappings, and internal behaviors using ADLs (AADL, MontiArc) and state machines (SysML v1.7, MontiArc Automaton)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcToolchain, SimulationExecutionEngine
]

instance ProductTechnicalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Product/technical layer representing physical structures, geometric compositions, and technical specifications using SysML IBDs and ADL component models."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingToolchain
]

instance BehavioralStateMachineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavior model defined using SysML v1.7 state machines or MontiArc Automaton for event-driven system responses and state transitions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcToolchain, SimulationExecutionEngine
]

instance PhysicsBasedSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based analytical model implemented in MathWorks Simulink or domain-specific simulation tools ([29], [30]) for performance analysis, stress testing, or failure mode simulations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationExecutionEngine
]

instance AggregatedContextualData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed and normalized contextual data traces from the physical system, filtered for noise reduction and feature extraction, serving as input for Digital Twin services."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PhysicalSystemContextualDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingPipeline, DigitalShadowAggregator
]

instance SimulationResultsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output from simulation engines (Simulink, domain-specific tools) containing predicted system behavior, performance metrics, and optimization outcomes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlAutomationService, OptimizationService
]

instance OperationalStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational state data from the physical system including sensor readings, actuator states, and environmental parameters."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PhysicalSystemContextualDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MonitoringService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingToolchain : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based toolchain supporting multi-layered system description (requirements, functional/logical architectures) and behavioral modeling (state machines)."
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemArchitectureDesignService, RequirementsTraceabilityService
]

instance MontiArcToolchain : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc toolchain for component-connector-based system decomposition, formal behavioral modeling (MontiArc Automaton), and heterogeneous tool integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentBasedDecompositionService, BehavioralSimulationService
]

instance SimulationExecutionEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Execution environment for dynamic system simulations (MathWorks Simulink, domain-specific tools) enabling real-time or near-real-time synchronization with physical systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionSimulationService, OptimizationService
]

instance MathWorksSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink platform for control system design, co-simulation, and physics-based analytical modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicsBasedSimulationService
]

instance DataProcessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware pipeline for aggregating, normalizing, and preprocessing heterogeneous data streams from physical systems into unified digital representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataInterpretationService
]

instance DigitalShadowAggregator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Passive digital shadow component collecting and abstracting contextual data traces from physical systems for purpose-driven analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionService
]

instance VersionControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Versioned repository system for managing MBSE models (SysML, ADLs) and ensuring traceability across system evolution."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelVersioningService
]

instance HeterogeneousToolIntegration : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration layer enabling cross-tool interoperability (SysML ↔ Simulink ↔ ADLs) to unify multi-notation models and stakeholder perspectives."
    DTDFVocab:enables MultiToolInteroperabilityService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataInterpretationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Acts as an active intermediary to interpret and process incoming data from the Digital Shadow, enabling real-time or near-real-time analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeSystemStateInsight, DataQualityAssessmentInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuously tracks the state and performance of the physical system via bidirectional or unidirectional data synchronization, leveraging model-based representations for anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemHealthInsight, OperationalAnomalyInsight
]

instance PredictionSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Utilizes integrated simulation engines to predict system behavior, optimize performance, or test scenarios under varying conditions (stress tests, failure modes)."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictedSystemBehaviorInsight, FailureModeAnalysisInsight
]

instance ControlAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implements autonomous decision-making to adjust physical system parameters or trigger corrective actions, closing the loop between digital and physical domains."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemControlInsight
]

instance VisualizationDecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides multi-perspective dashboards or interactive models (SysML diagrams, ADL visualizations) for diagnostics, planning, or maintenance scheduling."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemVisualizationInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Applies algorithmic or model-based optimization to improve efficiency, reduce costs, or enhance reliability of the physical system."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight
]

instance SystemArchitectureDesignService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supports engineers in design validation, requirements traceability, and functional/logical architecture analysis using MBSE methodologies."
    DTDFVocab:provides ArchitectureValidationInsight
]

instance RequirementsTraceabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Ensures traceability from system requirements through functional and logical architectures to product/technical implementations."
    DTDFVocab:provides RequirementsCoverageInsight
]

]

]

]

]

]

]

]

    base:desc "Automatically triggers corrective actions in response to detected anomalies or operational deviations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AdjustPhysicalParametersAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Modifies physical system parameters (e.g., actuator settings, process variables) based on digital twin recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OptimizeSystemPerformanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Applies optimization algorithms to adjust system configurations for improved efficiency or reliability."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ApplyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Executes optimization recommendations in the physical system, potentially requiring manual approval for critical changes."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Environment (System Under Study)
instance ComplexEngineeredSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Multi-layered complex engineered system (e.g., manufacturing/industrial system) modeled using MBSE methodologies (RFLP, OOSEM, SPES) with hierarchical abstraction layers."

                RequirementsModel,
                FunctionalArchitectureModel,
                LogicalArchitectureModel,
                ProductTechnicalModel,
                BehavioralStateMachineModel,
                PhysicsBasedSimulationModel,
                RealTimeControlAutomation,
                SimulationDrivenOptimization,
                MultiPerspectiveDashboardRenderer
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in this context is implicitly defined by **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, or SMArDT) and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like ACME, AADL, or MontiArc. The orchestration involves:
  1. **Multi-layered modeling**: Integration of **requirements, functional, logical, and physical architectures** using **component-connector perspectives** (e.g., SysML IBDs or MontiArc Automaton).
  2. **Tool heterogeneity**: Coordination across **diverse modeling tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink, C++/Java code) and **simulation environments** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink or other simulation tools [29, 30]).
  3. **Behavioral integration**: Combining **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **automata (MontiArc)** with **general-purpose code** for dynamic behavior.
  4. **Stakeholder alignment**: Ensuring **heterogeneous perspectives** (e.g., engineering, operations, maintenance) are harmonized via **shared model notations** and **tool interoperability**.
  The document does not specify **resource management** (e.g., cloud/edge allocation) or **system-wide coordination protocols**, but the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) implies a **passive data aggregation** role, suggesting that active orchestration (e.g., for DTs) would require **external active components** (e.g., software systems interpreting data flows [33, 34])."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **fidelity requirements** aligned with MBSE methodologies, where model accuracy is validated through **verification and validation (V&V) processes** tied to system abstraction layers. Uncertainty sources are quantified via:
  - **Model validation**: Cross-referenced against physical system behavior using simulation tools (e.g., Simulink) or code implementations (C++, Java) to detect discrepancies.
  - **Data validation**: Ensured through **automated one-way data flows** from physical objects (as defined in the Digital Shadow reference model [32]), where passive data collection is aggregated and abstracted for specific purposes. Error detection relies on **active components** (e.g., simulation engines or control logic) to interpret incoming data and flag inconsistencies.
  Quality assurance processes involve **multi-perspective integration** (e.g., combining SysML for structural fidelity with ADLs for behavioral correctness) and **stakeholder-specific validation** to address heterogeneous requirements. The system’s fidelity is constrained by the **granularity of modeling notations** (e.g., SysML IBDs for component-level accuracy) and the **precision of simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink for dynamic behavior). No explicit error correction mechanisms or uncertainty quantification metrics (e.g., tolerance thresholds) are specified in the document."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly detailed in the provided document. However, the **communication flow** between physical and digital components (as described in the digital shadow reference model) implies the need for **network protocols** capable of supporting **one-way data transmission** from physical objects to their digital counterparts. While specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, HTTP/REST, or industrial Ethernet) are not mentioned, the infrastructure must accommodate **heterogeneous data sources** (e.g., sensors, PLCs, or IoT devices) and integrate with **modeling tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink, or ADLs like MontiArc). The document does not specify **network topology** (e.g., star, mesh, or hierarchical), **bandwidth/latency requirements**, or **reliability/redundancy mechanisms**, though the passive nature of digital shadows suggests minimal real-time constraints. **Security protocols** (e.g., encryption, authentication) are not addressed, but the integration of heterogeneous stakeholders and systems [31] implies a need for secure communication channels."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly described in the provided document. However, the **modeling and simulation tools** referenced (e.g., SysML, Simulink, MontiArc, or general-purpose languages like C++/Java) suggest that the DT may be hosted on **hybrid platforms**, including **cloud** (for scalability and remote access), **edge** (for low-latency processing of sensor data), or **on-premise** (for legacy system integration). The document does not specify **computational/storage requirements**, **scalability mechanisms** (e.g., containerization, microservices), or **deployment models** (e.g., monolithic vs. modular). **Availability/reliability assurance** is implied by the need to support continuous data flows and stakeholder integration [31], but no concrete methods (e.g., failover, load balancing) are mentioned."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The document does not provide explicit details on **horizontal integration** with external systems. However, the **MBSE framework** (Section II.A) implies:
  - **Interoperability via modeling languages**: Systems are described using **standardized ADLs** (e.g., AADL, SysML) and **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink), which may support **data exchange protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, FMI, or proprietary APIs) for external system integration.
  - **Enterprise system dependencies**: The **multi-perspective modeling** (e.g., requirements, functional, physical layers) suggests integration with **ERP, MES, or PLM systems** via **shared data models** or **service-oriented architectures (SOA)**.
  - **Digital Twin/Digital Shadow boundaries**: The **one-way data flow** in digital shadows (Section II.B) implies **asynchronous integration** with physical systems, while DTs (as active entities) would require **synchronous or event-driven protocols** for bidirectional communication. However, **no specific standards (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST)** or **integration patterns** (e.g., microservices, event buses) are mentioned.
  The document does not address **dependencies on other Digital Twins** or **enterprise-wide coordination mechanisms**, as these are likely domain-specific and not covered in the provided text."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) standards** through the use of **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** for system architecture and behavior modeling, as well as **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **AADL, ACME, and MontiArc**, which are standardized or widely adopted in systems engineering. **Interoperability** is addressed via integration with **general-purpose programming languages (C++, Java)** and **simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation frameworks)**. While no explicit certification frameworks or compliance requirements are mentioned, the **UML-based Digital Shadow Reference Model (Fig. 1)** implies adherence to **UML 2.x standards** for class diagram representation. The document does not specify industry-specific standards (e.g., ISO 23227 for DTs, IEC 62443 for cybersecurity) or interoperability protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT), but the MBSE toolchain inherently supports **cross-tool integration** for heterogeneous stakeholder collaboration."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates **safety-critical system considerations** through the implementation of a **safety cell** restricting the operational range of the first vertical axis to **[-90°, 90°]**, ensuring physical confinement and collision avoidance. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implicitly supported by the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller**, which enforces operational limits (e.g., maximum speed of **|q̇_max| = 75 [°/s]**) and provides **real-time monitoring** of position, speed, and acceleration inputs/outputs. **Threat mitigation strategies** are partially addressed via the **dual-encoder sensor arrangement** (motor encoder + secondary encoder post-gearbox), which compensates for **backlash and positional uncertainty** by analyzing encoder discrepancies to infer **elasticity and friction** in the drivetrain. **Access control mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, but the CNC controller’s **separate axis control** and **input/output validation** (e.g., motor current conversion to torque via known motor constants) imply **authenticated operational boundaries**. No explicit mention of **cybersecurity protocols**, **data protection regulations**, **consent management**, or **data anonymization** is provided in the document."
]
